what_it_does: GC4 floor-triggered re-ask pass. For each class=='floor' entry in .build/rg10/reconciled.json (a dossier that failed the deterministic fact-check), it rebuilds the original element unit from the per-mockup artifact's cluster_members[0] and candidates.json ordering, re-fires the register_element role with the SAME grounding context PLUS the floor's named refcheck reasons as an explicit correction, then re-floors + re-juries (run_jury confirm_registration) + recomputes confirm_status, reclassifying the entry to confirmed / jury / floor. Updates reconciled.json in place (prior dossier kept under prior_dossier, reasked:true stamped). Then retires the prior status card and surfaces a final reconciled card.
observations: Imports heavily from registry_generation_run (_load_suite, _grounding_payload, _unit_of, EXEMPLAR_KEYS) and runtime.cognition, reusing the proven generation seams.
Builds a variant Role (reg_v) by copying register_element's spec and overriding input_addresses to ('utterance','ground','exemplars','inventory') — the bare-name ground chaining form.
Loads confirm_registration via importlib.util from roles/confirm_registration.py to call its confirm_status function.
Groups floor entries by source mockup so each group shares one grounding context, and processes them in batched run_items calls.
Correction text instructs: maps_to_feature must be copied verbatim from the allowed list or be literal 'proposed'; capabilities must use only allowed words.
Fail-loud handling: a re-ask whose output isn't a dict stays class='floor' and stamps reask_failed; unrebuildable cluster reps are recorded in 'skipped' with a reason, never silently dropped.
Re-classification: confirmed if cs['confirmed']; else 'floor' if any refcheck reason; else 'jury'.
Docstring 'THE FLOOR: proposes only; the operator's resolved field is never touched.'
Recounts all four classes and persists in place; docstring references a 'no-versioning law' (maximal capture, history visible).
standouts: The deterministic floor's rejection reason is fed back verbatim as correction — the rejection IS the repair signal.
Bounded single re-fire then re-gate: a re-asked dossier passes only by passing the SAME floor + jury, so the gate isn't weakened by the retry.
In-place update keeping prior_dossier and a reasked flag (no-versioning / maximal-capture discipline).
conventions: Family pattern: os.chdir to repo root, multiple sys.path inserts, read/write .build/rg10/reconciled.json, 'PROPOSES only / THE FLOOR' framing.
Reuse-don't-reimplement: leans on registry_generation_run helpers and runtime cognition rather than duplicating the chain.
Idempotency via stamps (reasked, reask_failed) so reruns are safe.
concerns: Mixes RoleRegistry-loaded confirm_registration (rr.get) for jury with an importlib-loaded copy of the same module for confirm_status — two load paths for one role file invite drift.
Heavy dynamic import / spec construction makes the data flow hard to trace statically.
Relies on artifact filename convention rg10-batch-<mockup-without-.html>.json; a mismatch silently routes the entry to 'skipped'.
notes: GC4 re-ask: feeds the deterministic floor's named rejection reasons back into one bounded re-generation, re-gates, and reclassifies floor entries in place — repairing fixable fabrications while leaving stubborn ones loudly flagged.
questions: Why is confirm_registration loaded twice (registry + importlib) rather than once — is confirm_status not exposed via the Role object?
What guarantees cluster_members[0] still maps to the same candidate ordering on a later run (candidates.json stability)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring describes a floor-triggered, bounded, re-gated re-ask that updates reconciled.json in place and resurfaces a card, which is what the code implements.
apparent_themes: Rejection-as-feedback (the floor's reason becomes the correction prompt)
Bounded retry behind an unchanged gate
In-place no-versioning capture with full history (prior_dossier)
Refine-before-gating / give Tim the smallest refined set
intention_signals: Automatically heal model fabrications that the deterministic floor caught, by re-prompting with the exact failure reason, without lowering the bar — so the human gate receives fewer, cleaner entries.
novelty: Closing the loop from a deterministic fact-check failure back into a single corrective re-generation that must re-pass the identical floor+jury, with the failure reason as the repair instruction.
contribution: Contributes the self-repair stage of the RG10 pipeline: a floor-feedback re-ask that converts fixable fabrications into confirmed entries and re-surfaces true counts, all as a proposal.
summary_for_embedding: A floor-triggered re-ask pass that rebuilds and re-fires register_element on fact-check-failed dossiers using the failure reason as correction, re-gates them through the same floor+jury, and updates reconciled.json in place.
intention_for_embedding: To automatically repair fixable registry fabrications by feeding the deterministic rejection reason back into one bounded re-generation behind an unchanged gate, shrinking what the human must review.
